page: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 14.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in memory-intensive and multi-threaded operations. The instance achieves strong ratings in large-block memory bandwidth for both read and write operations, as well as in the PassMark memory score. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations also rank in the top 25% of tested servers. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench fall within the average range, alongside average ratings for software compilation and ray tracing.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints without needing proportional scaling of compute cores. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage attachments, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. The hardware profile and benchmark strengths in database operations and memory bandwidth make this server suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, PassMark database operations, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, while single-core and multi-core CPU metrics remain in the average range. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by decoupling high RAM capacity from excessive compute scaling. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v5 is an ESv5 virtual machine with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48s_v5 is an ESv5 virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672.0 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM capacity and strong memory bandwidth for database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
672.0 GB of system memory providing 14.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong benchmark performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage bundled
Suited for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and static web serving
